items.title: Why consumers are forking out more for whiting, calamari and garfish

children.content: South Australia's peak seafood body says a short supply of some species due to the algal bloom and commercial fishing restrictions has led to slight price increases, as it warns it could take industry five to 10 years to fully recover.

gallery.altText: Fillets for sale at a fishmonger at the Adelaide Central Market.

thumbnail.altText: Fillets for sale at a fishmonger at the Adelaide Central Market.

items.title: Caravan repairers battling to stay afloat as travellers stay close to home

children.content: Normally at this time of year, RVs are hitting the road to head north and escape winter. But the ongoing fuel crisis is forcing travellers to stay closer to home, causing a serious economic downturn for many of the 2,500 businesses in the RV repair industry.

image.altText: RVs at a caravan park

items.title: Man charged with five counts of rape following alleged Adelaide Hills break-in

children.content: A man accused of breaking into an Adelaide Hills home and sexually assaulting a person unknown to him has been charged with multiple counts of rape, court documents show.

image.altText: An image of the front of the Adelaide Magistrates Court building on Victoria Square

items.title: Pauline Hanson caught swearing as staffer says 'shut up' to journalist

children.content: Pauline Hanson and her party have again been caught in tense moments with media, with the One Nation leader captured on camera swearing and a staffer telling a journalist to "shut up".

gallery.altText: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson at a media conference.

thumbnail.altText: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson at a media conference.

items.title: 'Emotional' Hawks kick off Tassie farewell as star Crow injured

children.content: Blake Hardwick emerges the unlikeliest of heroes for Hawthorn as the Hawks record a nine-point win over Adelaide in Tasmania.

image.altText: Blake Hardwick raises both arms in the air in celebration

items.title: 'Grossly intoxicated' lawyer's fatal hit-run crash jail term reduced on appeal

children.content: An Adelaide lawyer who drank 1.1 litres of wine before killing a pedestrian in a fatal hit-run crash has won a reduced sentence on appeal — in part because she has a young child.

image.altText: A close up of Lauren Willgoose with a serious expression on her face

items.title: Government doing 'everything in our power' to keep SA safe from notorious paedophile

children.content: The South Australian government has filed two applications to either ensure notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy is indefinitely detained or the subject of an extended supervision order.

image.altText: Notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy.

items.title: Angus Taylor spruiks gas extraction as SA Liberals oppose fracking bid

children.content: After telling an Adelaide oil and gas conference "we need to get busy digging and drilling", Opposition Leader Angus Taylor bats away questions about the SA Liberal Party's resistance to fracking in the state's South East.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie speaks with a woman in the background and a blue banner for Australian Energy Producers

items.title: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image has been revealed — here's why

children.content: The youngest of the Snowtown killers was the subject of a long-running suppression order that kept his face — or any description of his appearance — hidden for 25 years. Here's why that's been lifted.

articles.title: Snowtown killer's suppression order lifts after 25 years

children.content: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image can be revealed after South Australia's highest court lifted a suppression order made 25 years ago.

image.altText: A court sketch of Snowtown killer James Vlassakis.

articles.title: SA Parole Board says commissioner made 'error' in overturning Snowtown killer's release

children.content: New documents accuse the Parole Administrative Review Commissioner of making a mistake by not allowing the SA Parole Board to participate in a review of a decision to release Snowtown serial killer James Vlassakis.

image.altText: The old bank at the township of Snowtown.

gallery.altText: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis in custody.

thumbnail.altText: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis in custody.

items.title: X fights Andrew and Tristan Tate's bid to unmask anonymous critics

children.content: Misogynist influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate have struck a hurdle in their bid to unmask anonymous users they allege have defamed them, with social media platform X asking a Florida court to dismiss the motion.

image.altText: two men standing near each other

items.title: Outback landholders opening gate to large-scale renewable projects

children.content: If you mention renewable energy to agricultural producers in many regional communities, you will likely ruffle some feathers. But in far west NSW, landholders are showing preliminary support for a planned expansion of solar and wind energy assets.

image.altText: Infant boy sits on gate with his dad in outback arid location. Dad is wearing pink shirt and jeans.

items.title: Widespread infectious disease outbreak labelled public health 'failure'

children.content: The federal government has stepped in to address the largest diphtheria outbreak since records began, unveiling a multi-million dollar package for more vaccines and workers to deliver them.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit and tie looks sternly into camera.

items.title: What we know about Iran's new system of transit in the Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Iran is asserting unprecedented control over the strategically critical waterway, with ships reportedly facing screening processes, transit approvals and potential Bitcoin-backed insurance requirements.

image.altText: A drone view shows the Malta-flagged tanker Agios Fanourios I arriving in Iraq's territorial waters off Basra.

items.title: 'Is that a UFO?' Suspected meteor lights up skies across NSW

children.content: Colourful streaks across the skies have been spotted from Bondi to Canberra as a "fireball" meteor passed through the atmosphere overnight.

image.altText: Dashcam vision shows bright light from a meteor over the road at night.

gallery.altText: Dashcam footage showing a flashing appearing across the sky

thumbnail.altText: Dashcam footage showing a flashing appearing across the sky

items.title: Snowy bosses paid $1.2m in bonuses as hydro project costs spiral

children.content: The renewables project's chief executive, Dennis Barnes, received $323,000 in bonus pay on top of his $1.69 million salary last financial year, while four other senior executives received about $919,000 in "variable pay".

image.altText: A man wearing a construction outfit, speaking at a lectern.

items.title: One Nation surfs wave as Labor defends budget

children.content: Before the budget, Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers knew they would be hit by criticism for its broken promises, but they underestimated the extent and ferocity of the backlash.

image.altText: A man in an Akubara wraps his arms around a woman with a microphone

items.title: Chalmers asked to explain how he 'landed on 30 per cent' tax rate

children.content: The Productivity Commission was not asked to model how changes to capital gains tax would impact Australia's economy. Business groups say Treasurer Jim Chalmers must explain how the government concluded its tax changes would ultimately be a boon to productivity.

image.altText: Jim Chalmers speaks to reporters.

items.title: Half Australia's farms are in trusts. So what does tax reform mean for farmers?

children.content: Despite winning a carve-out to changes to family trusts announced in the budget, farmers are still worried about what tax reform means for them.

image.altText: people working in a field

items.title: Cuba on the brink: Residents describe life 'trapped between two psychopaths'

children.content: As power and petrol supplies run out, Cubans tell the ABC about their daily lives. "You can no longer find streets packed with travellers, music and dance," one says. "Only Cuban nationals, still attempting to take some bread home."

articles.title: Cuba accepts offer of $140m in American aid, Rubio says

children.content: Tensions between the longtime Cold War rivals have hit a new low in recent days after the US indicted the country's 94-year-old former president Raúl Castro.

image.altText: Marco Rubio speaking to reporters outside.

gallery.altText: A man rides a motorbike with a woman on the back beside crumbling buildings.
A boy jumps from rocks into the ocean as other children sit and watch.
A woman rides a bike beside a collapsed building.

thumbnail.altText: A man rides a motorbike with a woman on the back beside crumbling buildings.
A boy jumps from rocks into the ocean as other children sit and watch.
A woman rides a bike beside a collapsed building.

avatar.alt: A headshot of ABC producer Emilie Gramenz, wearing a checked blazer

items.title: Parents of children in foster care say 'black eyes' and bite marks were ignored

children.content: The parents of two children in Northern Territory foster care have spoken out about their "heartbreaking" experience with the system, saying concerns their daughter was being abused by her foster family were brushed off by the department.

image.altText: A woman in a sleeveless, long dress, long brown hair in kitchen, blurred background, with brown teddy bear focus in foreground.

items.title: In 1990 there were 599 million mental disorder cases globally. Now there are 1.2 billion

children.content: A surge over the last three decades means mental disorders now account for more than 17 per cent of all disability worldwide, according to a report published this week.

image.altText: illustration of a woman's face cut into slides on an orange background

items.title: The Mushroom Tapes, Mad Mabel and Memorial Days triumph at book awards

children.content: In May 2025, Helen Garner, Chloe Hooper and Sarah Krasnostein, three literary true-crime writers, headed to Erin Patterson's trial. The resulting book just won an Australian Book Industry Award.

gallery.altText: Sarah Krasnostein, 48, and Chloe Hooper, 52, sit on either side of a table. Helen Garner, 83, stands between them.

thumbnail.altText: Sarah Krasnostein, 48, and Chloe Hooper, 52, sit on either side of a table. Helen Garner, 83, stands between them.

avatar.alt: Hannah Story, a blonde woman sitting outside smiles. A dog is at her feet, and she wears a T-shirt that reads "MIRANDA JULY".

items.title: The hidden codes and Aussie influence in Harry Connick Jr's new symphony

children.content: Harry Connick Jr's new symphony, Elaboratio, is filled with musical tributes to his mother Anita. Orchestra Victoria and Australian conductor Jessica Gethin are bringing those memories to life at Carnegie Hall.

image.altText: A man in a black jacket with an earpiece holds a microphone with a red and purple background

items.title: Friday news quiz: A pumped prince, a grinning sculpture and a stowaway frog

children.content: You'll be as happy as Prince William when you take this week's quiz, which goes off like a frog in a
bag of lettuce.

image.altText: Kylie Minogue sings into a microphone while wearing a rainbow-coloured dress.

items.title: Sydney council slaps dog walkers and gender reveals with new park fees

children.content: A picnic vendor told the ABC they found out about the proposed fees through a group chat with other small businesses, and fears the proposed fee structure could "kill an entire industry".

gallery.altText: A woman sits on a picnic rug with a view of Sydney Harbour behind her.

thumbnail.altText: A woman sits on a picnic rug with a view of Sydney Harbour behind her.

items.title: Soft plastics industry at breaking point, as waste instead sent overseas

children.content: The soft plastic recycling industry wants more waste to stay here, saying too much material is being sent overseas.

image.altText: A white haired man with glasses, a blue collared shirt and bright yellow vest staring at piles of plastic while holding a piece.

items.title: US-bound plane diverted after passenger from Ebola-hit Congo boards 'in error'

children.content: The US has introduced new restrictions on travellers from countries exposed to an Ebola outbreak in Africa, requiring them to enter through Washington DC.

image.altText: An Air France plane taxiing.

items.title: Air France and Airbus fined for 2009 crash that killed 228

children.content: France's national airline Air France and Airbus have both been found guilty of involuntary manslaughter over a 2009 crash that killed 228 people.

image.altText: An Air France plane taxiing.

items.title: UK trade staff told to be 'careful and sometimes strict' in dealing with Andrew

children.content: The historic documents show no security vetting took place at the time.

image.altText: Prince Andrew and Queen Elizabeth looking serious as they stand at a balcony decorated with flowers

items.title: Father of flotilla detainee makes serious allegations against Israel during live interview

children.content: Israel's ambassador to Australia Hillel Newman has stopped short of apologising to the Global Sumud Flotilla activists detained in Israel who had been taunted by far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

articles.title: Israel supported Ben-Gvir's callousness, until it didn't

children.content: Benjamin Netanyahu has condemned a video of his national security minister taunting activists, including Australians, who have been detained. But it's the prime minister who allowed this behaviour to percolate.

image.altText: Ben-Gvir looks furious as he points at prisoners during a visit.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit stands in a corridor of parliament house.

gallery.altText: Two young men and a young woman look seriously at the camera inside a grey-walled room.
three people in life jackets on a boat

thumbnail.altText: Two young men and a young woman look seriously at the camera inside a grey-walled room.
three people in life jackets on a boat

items.title: 'Absolute shock': NASCAR champion Kyle Busch dies aged 41

children.content: Kyle Busch, who won two Cup Series championships and will be a future NASCAR Hall of Famer, has died aged 41.

image.altText: A man in racing clothing smiling.

items.title: Early lung damage in kids with CF linked with immune cell changes

children.content: Australian researchers have mapped how lung damage may begin early in life for children with cystic fibrosis, providing new insights that may help reshape future care.

image.altText: A boy does a lung test in a room.

items.title: What is 'pink slime' journalism and has it infiltrated Australian media?

children.content: Experts say a series of AI-generated digital news outlets is the first recorded example of "pink slime" journalism in Australia, with the website owner claiming the now-deleted outlets were an experiment gone wrong.

image.altText: A hand holding a phone wiht the bunbury guardian home page displayed on the screen with a pink background ebhind the phone

items.title: AI-generated resumes making hiring 'incredibly difficult', recruiters say

children.content: AI-perfected CVs are becoming the norm as more job applicants and employers use artificial intelligence, but recruiters say the technology could be making hiring trickier.

image.altText: A close up of text on a computer screen over a woman's shoulder.

items.title: Dismay, anger over 1870s mansion reduced to rubble

children.content: How did a colonial-era mansion once earmarked to be on the state heritage register get bulldozed?

gallery.altText: a blue excavator digs at a pile of old timber and brick
An old black and white photograph of a house at 180 George Street, Launceston.
